meta.name: framer-clone
meta.goal: Clone Framer no-code website builder — canvas editor, drag-and-drop, component system, responsive preview

tasks.action: Scaffold project with React 19 + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS + Vitest + Framer Motion + Zustand
steps: Create .playground/framer-clone/ with Vite React-TS template
Install dependencies: react, react-dom, framer-motion, zustand, immer, tailwindcss
Install dev dependencies: vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom, happy-dom
Configure Tailwind CSS with dark theme colors matching Framer editor aesthetic
Configure Vitest with happy-dom environment
Verify: npm run build succeeds, npm run dev starts without errors
tasks.summary: Scaffolded project with React 19 + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS v4 + Vitest + Framer Motion + Zustand

tasks.action: Implement Zustand store with element tree, selection state, and undo/redo via Immer
steps: Define element types (Frame, Text, Image, Button, Input, Video, Icon, Divider) with shared base props
Create Zustand store with Immer middleware for immutable state updates
Implement element CRUD: addElement, removeElement, updateElement, moveElement
Implement selection state: selectedIds, selectElement, deselectAll, multiSelect
Implement undo/redo with history stack using temporal Zustand middleware pattern
Write unit tests for store operations and undo/redo
tasks.summary: Implemented Zustand store with element tree, selection, undo/redo via Immer

tasks.action: Implement 3-panel editor layout with toolbar, left panel (layers/components tabs), canvas area, and right panel (properties)
steps: Create EditorLayout component with CSS Grid: toolbar row + 3-column body (left, canvas, right)
Implement collapsible left/right panels with resize handles
Create Toolbar component with tool selection slots, zoom controls, undo/redo buttons
Add left panel tab switching between Layers and Components views
Style with Tailwind using dark theme (Framer-like aesthetic: dark gray backgrounds, subtle borders)
tasks.summary: Implemented 3-panel editor layout with toolbar, left/right panels, canvas area

tasks.action: Implement DOM-based canvas with pan/zoom using CSS transforms
steps: Create Canvas component with a transform container for pan/zoom
Implement pan: Space+drag or middle-mouse drag to pan the canvas
Implement zoom: Cmd+scroll or pinch to zoom, centered on cursor position
Add zoom limits (10% to 500%) and zoom level display in toolbar
Render element tree from Zustand store as DOM nodes with absolute positioning
Write tests for zoom limits and pan behavior
tasks.summary: Implemented DOM-based Canvas with pan/zoom via CSS transforms

tasks.action: Implement element selection, resize handles, and drag-to-move on canvas
steps: Implement click-to-select and click-on-empty-to-deselect
Add multi-select via Shift+click and marquee selection
Create resize handles (8 points: corners + midpoints) on selected elements
Implement drag-to-resize with Framer Motion drag gestures
Implement drag-to-move selected elements, maintaining relative positions for multi-select
Add minimum size constraint (1x1px) during resize
tasks.summary: Implemented element selection, resize handles, and drag-to-move on canvas

tasks.action: Implement component library panel with 8 types in 4 categories and drag-to-canvas
steps: Create ComponentLibrary component with 4 category sections (Layout/Content/Form/Media)
Define default props for each of the 8 component types
Implement drag from library using Framer Motion drag or HTML5 drag API
Handle drop on canvas: create new element at drop position via store
Add placeholder rendering for Image (gray box) and Video (play icon) when no source
Write tests for all 8 component types rendering
tasks.summary: Implemented component library drag-to-canvas with createDefaultElement factory

tasks.action: Implement layers panel with tree view, drag-to-reorder, and nesting
steps: Create LayersPanel component rendering element tree recursively
Show element type icons and names with indentation for nesting depth
Implement click-to-select synced with canvas selection
Implement drag-to-reorder within the tree (change z-order and parent)
Add expand/collapse for container elements
Show empty state when canvas has no elements
tasks.summary: Implemented LayersPanel with tree view, drag-to-reorder, expand/collapse

tasks.action: Implement properties panel with layout, style, and effects sections
steps: Create PropertiesPanel component with collapsible sections: Layout, Style, Effects
Layout section: position (x, y), size (width, height), rotation, overflow
Style section: background color, border, border-radius, opacity, font properties (for Text)
Effects section: box-shadow, blur (basic)
All inputs sync bidirectionally with store — editing updates canvas in real-time
Show empty state when no element selected
Validate numeric inputs: reject non-numeric, clamp negative dimensions
tasks.summary: Implemented PropertiesPanel with Layout, Style, and Effects sections

tasks.action: Implement responsive preview with breakpoint switching (desktop/tablet/mobile)
steps: Add breakpoint switcher to toolbar: Desktop (1440px), Tablet (768px), Mobile (375px)
On breakpoint switch, resize the canvas viewport frame to the selected width
Elements within the viewport should reflow or clip based on their container
Store selected breakpoint in editor state
tasks.summary: Implemented responsive preview with breakpoint switching (desktop/tablet/mobile)

tasks.action: Implement animation configuration panel (hover effects + CSS transitions)
steps: Add Animation section to properties panel (below Effects)
Implement hover effect configuration: target properties (scale, opacity, x, y, rotation)
Add transition settings: duration (ms), easing (ease, ease-in, ease-out, ease-in-out, spring)
Store animation config per element in the store
Show disabled state when no element selected
tasks.summary: Implemented animation configuration panel (hover effects + CSS transitions)

tasks.action: Implement LocalStorage persistence, auto-save, and JSON export/import
steps: Implement auto-save to LocalStorage on store changes (debounced, 2s)
Implement load from LocalStorage on app startup
Add Export JSON button that triggers file download
Add Import JSON with file picker and schema validation
Show error notification for invalid imports
Handle LocalStorage full/unavailable gracefully
tasks.summary: Implemented LocalStorage persistence, auto-save, and JSON export/import

tasks.action: Implement element grouping and custom component creation
steps: Add group/ungroup actions to store (Cmd+G / Cmd+Shift+G)
Group creates a new Frame-like container element with children
Implement Save as Component action that registers the group in custom components list
Show custom components in the component library under a Custom category
Allow dragging custom components from library to canvas (creates a copy)
Disable group when 0 or 1 elements selected
tasks.summary: Implemented element grouping and custom component creation

tasks.action: Full verification — build, lint, and all test suites
steps: Run full build
Run linter
Run all test suites
Verify dev server starts without errors
tasks.summary: All 208 tests pass, build clean, lint clean

tasks.action: Implement keyboard shortcuts system (Delete, Copy, Paste, Cut, Duplicate, Select All, Deselect)
steps: Create useKeyboardShortcuts hook that listens for keyboard events at document level
Implement Delete/Backspace: remove selected elements from store
Implement Cmd+C/V/X: copy/paste/cut via internal clipboard (store-based, not system clipboard)
Implement Cmd+D: duplicate in-place with offset
Implement Cmd+A: select all elements
Implement Esc: deselect all
Suppress shortcuts when text input is focused (text editing mode)
tasks.summary: Implemented keyboard shortcuts (Delete, Copy, Paste, Cut, Duplicate, Select All, Deselect)

tasks.action: Implement snap guides and align/distribute actions
steps: Calculate snap points from all non-selected elements (left, right, top, bottom, center edges)
During drag/resize, detect proximity (5px threshold) to snap points
Render visual guide lines (thin colored lines) at snap positions
Implement align actions: left, center, right, top, middle, bottom
Implement distribute actions: horizontal, vertical (requires 3+ selected elements)
Add align/distribute buttons to toolbar (active only when 2+ elements selected)
tasks.summary: Implemented snap guides and align/distribute actions

tasks.action: Implement style presets (color palette, font presets, spacing presets)
steps: Define preset data: color palette (6-8 colors), font presets (Heading L/M/S, Body L/M/S), spacing presets (4/8/12/16/24/32)
Create StylePresets component in properties panel with color swatches, font preset buttons, spacing selector
Apply preset: update only the relevant style properties, preserve others
Show disabled state when no element is selected
tasks.summary: Implemented style presets (color palette, font presets, spacing presets)

history.summary: AnimationPanel created with hover effect + transition config; store extended with animationConfigs; all 13 tests pass; build passes

context.request: Framer 웹사이트 빌더 서비스 클론 코딩
context.confirmed_goal: Framer의 핵심 서비스인 노코드 웹사이트 빌더를 클론 코딩한다. 캔버스 기반 비주얼 에디터, 드래그앤드롭 요소 배치, 컴포넌트 트리/레이어 패널, 속성 편집 패널, 반응형 프리뷰, 기본 애니메이션 설정을 포함하는 웹 기반 디자인 툴을 구현한다.
context.research: Framer는 노코드 웹사이트 빌더. 핵심 기능: 캔버스 기반 비주얼 에디터, 드래그앤드롭, 컴포넌트 시스템, 레이어 패널, 속성 편집, 반응형 디자인 프리뷰, 애니메이션/인터랙션 설정. 기존 repo pattern: React 19 + Vite 8 in .playground/. Build: dev=vite, build=vite build, lint=eslint.

decisions.decision: React 19 + Vite 8 + Tailwind CSS for UI framework
decisions.rationale: Matches existing playground pattern, fast dev cycle, utility-first CSS for rapid styling

decisions.decision: Framer Motion for animations and interactions
decisions.rationale: Framer's own animation library, closest to original behavior, excellent drag gesture support

decisions.decision: Zustand + Immer for state management with undo/redo
decisions.rationale: Lightweight, minimal boilerplate, immer middleware enables immutable undo/redo history
Framer actual approach: real DOM elements on canvas with CSS transforms for pan/zoom, enables WYSIWYG editing

decisions.decision: LocalStorage + JSON export for data persistence
decisions.rationale: No backend needed, simple serialization, user can export/import projects as JSON files

decisions.decision: Animation scope: hover effects and CSS transitions only (no scroll trigger, no timeline editor)
decisions.rationale: MVP scope — hover effect + opacity/scale/position transitions with easing covers core Framer interaction patterns

decisions.decision: 8 base component types: Frame(Container), Text, Image, Button, Input, Video, Icon, Divider
decisions.rationale: Covers all essential web building blocks. Frame is the core layout container (like Framer's Frame).

decisions.decision: 3-panel editor layout: Left (Layers + Component Library) | Center (Canvas) | Right (Properties Panel) | Top (Toolbar)
decisions.rationale: Matches Framer's actual UI layout. Left panel has layers tree and component library tabs. Right panel has layout/style/effects/animation sections. Top toolbar has tool selection, zoom controls, undo/redo.

alternatives_rejected.reason: Too limited, doesn't match Framer's UX

decisions.decision: Components added to canvas via drag-and-drop from library panel
decisions.rationale: Matches Framer's UX pattern — intuitive spatial placement via drag gesture

decisions.decision: Support custom components via element grouping — group elements into reusable components
decisions.rationale: Key Framer feature — users can group elements and reuse them as custom components across the project

decisions.decision: Component library organized in 4 categories: Layout, Content, Form, Media
decisions.rationale: Clear semantic grouping — Frame in Layout, Text+Button in Content, Input in Form, Image+Video+Icon+Divider in Media

alternatives_rejected.reason: Hard to find components at scale

requirements.behavior: Users can drag and drop elements from the component library onto the canvas to place them at the drop position

scenarios.given: The editor is open with an empty canvas and the component library is visible
scenarios.when: The user drags a Frame component from the library and drops it onto the canvas
scenarios.then: A Frame element appears on the canvas at the drop position and is selected

scenarios.given: The editor is open with the canvas visible
scenarios.when: The user drags a component but releases outside the canvas area
scenarios.then: No element is added to the canvas and no error occurs

scenarios.given: The canvas already has 50+ elements
scenarios.when: The user drags another component onto the canvas
scenarios.then: The element is added successfully without noticeable lag

checks: New element appears within 500ms of drop

requirements.behavior: Users can select an element and edit its visual properties (size, position, color, text, border, opacity, etc.) via the properties panel

scenarios.given: A Text element exists on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user selects the element and changes its font size to 24px and color to red in the properties panel
scenarios.then: The element on the canvas reflects the updated font size and color immediately

scenarios.given: No element is selected on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user views the properties panel
scenarios.then: The properties panel shows a placeholder or empty state, no editable fields

scenarios.given: A Frame element is selected
scenarios.when: The user enters a negative width value (-100) in the size field
scenarios.then: The input is rejected or clamped to the minimum allowed value (e.g., 0 or 1)

scenarios.given: A Frame element is selected
scenarios.when: The user enters non-numeric text 'abc' into the width field
scenarios.then: The input is rejected and the previous valid value is preserved

requirements.behavior: Users can view and manage element hierarchy via a layers panel showing a tree structure with drag-to-reorder and nesting

scenarios.given: The canvas has a Frame containing a Text and an Image element
scenarios.when: The user views the layers panel
scenarios.then: The panel shows a tree with Frame as parent and Text/Image as children

scenarios.given: The layers panel shows multiple elements
scenarios.when: The user drags an element in the layers panel to reorder it
scenarios.then: The element moves to the new position in the tree and the canvas z-order updates accordingly

scenarios.given: The canvas is empty
scenarios.when: The user views the layers panel
scenarios.then: The panel shows an empty state message

scenarios.given: The layers panel has a deeply nested tree (5+ levels)
scenarios.when: The user expands all nodes
scenarios.then: All levels are visible with proper indentation

requirements.behavior: Users can undo and redo editing actions via keyboard shortcuts (Cmd+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z) or toolbar buttons

scenarios.given: The user has moved an element on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user presses Cmd+Z
scenarios.then: The element returns to its previous position

scenarios.given: The user has undone an action
scenarios.when: The user presses Cmd+Shift+Z
scenarios.then: The action is redone and the element returns to the modified state

scenarios.given: No actions have been performed yet (empty history)
scenarios.when: The user presses Cmd+Z
scenarios.then: Nothing happens and undo button appears disabled

scenarios.given: The user has performed multiple edits
scenarios.when: The user performs a new action after undoing
scenarios.then: The redo history is cleared and only undo of the new action is available

requirements.behavior: Users can preview their design at different responsive breakpoints (desktop 1440px, tablet 768px, mobile 375px)

scenarios.given: A design with elements is on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user switches to tablet breakpoint (768px) via toolbar
scenarios.then: The canvas viewport resizes to 768px width and elements reflow accordingly

scenarios.given: The canvas is empty
scenarios.when: The user switches to mobile breakpoint
scenarios.then: The viewport resizes to 375px but no error occurs

scenarios.given: Design has elements wider than mobile viewport
scenarios.when: The user switches to mobile breakpoint (375px)
scenarios.then: Elements that overflow are visible with horizontal scroll or clipped appropriately

checks: Overflow elements are handled gracefully (clipped or scrollable)

requirements.behavior: Users can save projects to LocalStorage automatically and export/import projects as JSON files

scenarios.given: The user has created elements on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user clicks Save or the auto-save triggers
scenarios.then: The project state is persisted to LocalStorage

scenarios.given: A project has been saved
scenarios.when: The user clicks Export JSON
scenarios.then: A JSON file is downloaded containing the complete project state

scenarios.given: The user has a previously exported JSON file
scenarios.when: The user clicks Import and selects the JSON file
scenarios.then: The project is loaded and the canvas displays the imported elements

scenarios.given: The user attempts to import a file
scenarios.when: The file is not valid JSON or has an invalid schema
scenarios.then: An error message is shown and the current project is not affected

scenarios.given: LocalStorage is full or unavailable
scenarios.when: Auto-save triggers
scenarios.then: A warning notification is shown suggesting JSON export as backup

scenarios.given: The user imports a file that is valid JSON but missing required fields (e.g., no elements array, wrong schema version)
scenarios.when: The import is attempted
scenarios.then: A specific schema validation error is shown and the current project is not affected

requirements.behavior: Users can configure hover effects and CSS transitions (opacity, scale, position, rotation) on elements via the animation section of the properties panel

scenarios.given: A Button element is selected
scenarios.when: The user adds a hover effect with scale 1.1 and opacity 0.8 in the animation panel
scenarios.then: The configuration is saved and visible in the animation section

scenarios.given: No element is selected
scenarios.when: The user views the animation section
scenarios.then: The animation section is disabled or shows empty state

scenarios.given: An element has a hover effect configured
scenarios.when: The user sets transition duration to 0ms
scenarios.then: The transition is instant (no animation) but no error occurs

requirements.behavior: The component library provides 8 base component types (Frame, Text, Image, Button, Input, Video, Icon, Divider) organized in 4 categories

scenarios.given: The editor is open
scenarios.when: The user opens the component library panel
scenarios.then: All 8 component types are visible grouped into Layout, Content, Form, and Media categories

scenarios.given: The component library is open
scenarios.when: The user drags each of the 8 component types onto the canvas
scenarios.then: Each component renders with its default appearance and properties

scenarios.given: An Image component is added to the canvas
scenarios.when: No image source is provided
scenarios.then: A placeholder image is displayed

scenarios.given: A Video component is added to the canvas
scenarios.when: An invalid video URL is provided
scenarios.then: An error placeholder is shown instead of a broken video element

requirements.behavior: The canvas supports pan (scroll/drag) and zoom (pinch/scroll+modifier) to navigate large designs

scenarios.given: The canvas has content extending beyond the viewport
scenarios.when: The user holds Space and drags on the canvas
scenarios.then: The canvas pans in the drag direction

scenarios.given: The canvas is at default zoom
scenarios.when: The user scrolls with Cmd held
scenarios.then: The canvas zooms in/out centered on the cursor position

scenarios.given: The canvas is at maximum zoom (500%)
scenarios.when: The user tries to zoom in further
scenarios.then: The zoom level stays at maximum and does not exceed the limit

scenarios.given: The canvas is at minimum zoom (10%)
scenarios.when: The user tries to zoom out further
scenarios.then: The zoom level stays at minimum

scenarios.given: The user is panning the canvas by holding Space and dragging
scenarios.when: The user releases Space while still holding the mouse button
scenarios.then: The pan stops and the cursor returns to the default selection tool without unexpected behavior

requirements.behavior: Users can resize and reposition elements directly on the canvas via selection handles and drag gestures

scenarios.given: A Frame element is selected on the canvas showing resize handles
scenarios.when: The user drags the bottom-right handle
scenarios.then: The element resizes proportionally and the new dimensions are reflected in the properties panel

scenarios.given: A Frame element is selected
scenarios.when: The user drags the element body
scenarios.then: The element moves to the new position and coordinates update in properties panel

scenarios.given: A small element (10x10px) is selected
scenarios.when: The user tries to resize it smaller than minimum (e.g., 1x1px)
scenarios.then: The element size is clamped to the minimum allowed size

scenarios.given: Multiple elements are selected
scenarios.when: The user drags to move them
scenarios.then: All selected elements move together maintaining their relative positions

requirements.behavior: The editor displays a 3-panel layout with left panel (layers + component library), center canvas, right panel (properties), and top toolbar

scenarios.given: The user opens the editor
scenarios.when: The application loads
scenarios.then: The editor displays left panel, center canvas, right panel, and top toolbar

scenarios.given: The left panel is visible
scenarios.when: The user switches between Layers and Components tabs
scenarios.then: The panel content toggles between the layers tree and component library

scenarios.given: The editor is open on a narrow viewport (1024px)
scenarios.when: The application renders
scenarios.then: All three panels remain visible with reduced widths, maintaining usability

checks: All three panels are visible at 1024px viewport

scenarios.given: The editor is open
scenarios.when: The user collapses the left or right panel
scenarios.then: The panel collapses and the canvas expands to fill the space

requirements.behavior: Users can select multiple elements and group them into a reusable custom component that appears in the component library

scenarios.given: Two or more elements are selected on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user triggers Group (Cmd+G or context menu)
scenarios.then: The elements are grouped into a single component, visible as a group in the layers panel

scenarios.given: A custom component group exists
scenarios.when: The user saves it as a reusable component
scenarios.then: The component appears in the component library under a Custom category

scenarios.given: Only one element is selected
scenarios.when: The user tries to group
scenarios.then: The group action is disabled or shows a message that multiple elements are required

scenarios.given: A grouped component is on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user triggers Ungroup (Cmd+Shift+G)
scenarios.then: The group is dissolved and individual elements are restored to their original state

requirements.behavior: Users can perform common editing actions via keyboard shortcuts: Delete (Del/Backspace), Copy (Cmd+C), Paste (Cmd+V), Cut (Cmd+X), Duplicate (Cmd+D), Select All (Cmd+A), and Deselect (Esc)

scenarios.given: An element is selected on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user presses Delete or Backspace
scenarios.then: The selected element is removed from the canvas and layers panel

scenarios.given: An element is selected
scenarios.when: The user presses Cmd+C then Cmd+V
scenarios.then: A duplicate of the element is created with a slight offset from the original

scenarios.given: No element is selected
scenarios.when: The user presses Delete
scenarios.then: Nothing happens and no error is thrown

scenarios.given: The user is editing text inline in a Text element
scenarios.when: The user presses Delete
scenarios.then: The text character is deleted, NOT the element itself (shortcut is suppressed during text editing)

requirements.behavior: The canvas shows alignment guides (snap lines) when elements are moved or resized near other elements' edges or centers, and provides align/distribute actions for multiple selected elements

scenarios.given: Two elements exist on the canvas
scenarios.when: The user drags one element until its left edge aligns with the other element's left edge
scenarios.then: A vertical snap guide appears and the element snaps to the aligned position

checks: A visible vertical guide line appears at the alignment point
The dragged element snaps to the aligned position

scenarios.given: Three or more elements are selected
scenarios.when: The user triggers 'Distribute Horizontally' from the toolbar
scenarios.then: The elements are evenly spaced horizontally

scenarios.given: Only one element is selected
scenarios.when: The user triggers an align action
scenarios.then: The align action is disabled or aligns relative to the canvas/parent frame

scenarios.given: An element is being resized near another element's edge
scenarios.when: The element's new edge comes within 5px of the other element
scenarios.then: A snap guide appears and the resize snaps to the nearby edge

checks: Snap guide appears during resize operation
Resize snaps to the nearby element edge

requirements.behavior: Users can apply style presets (color palette, font selection, spacing presets) to quickly style elements with consistent design tokens

scenarios.given: A Frame element is selected
scenarios.when: The user selects a color from the color palette preset (e.g., primary blue)
scenarios.then: The element's background color updates to the preset color value

scenarios.given: A Text element is selected
scenarios.when: The user selects a font preset (e.g., Heading Large)
scenarios.then: The element's font family, size, weight, and line-height update to match the preset

scenarios.given: A Text element has custom styles applied
scenarios.when: The user applies a font preset
scenarios.then: The preset values override the custom styles for the preset fields only, other styles are preserved

scenarios.given: No element is selected
scenarios.when: The user views the style presets panel
scenarios.then: Presets are visible but disabled or show a prompt to select an element first
